animes.name: IS: Infinite Stratos Encore - Koi ni Kogareru Rokujuusou
animes.alt_name: IS: Infinite Stratos Encore - Koi ni Kogareru Rokujuusou

rating.explain:
Before you read this, remember: I love this Anime and I'm very biased towards it. Also go watch it now. Or atleast give me the chance to convince you doing so. Be careful though, you might get diabetes from cuteness overload.

Ano Natsu de Matteru or Waiting in the Summer or WITS, how I like to call it, like the Studio Wit, is a fantastic anime (this is the short version of the review, thank you for reading). There's barely anything more beautiful than a well-written romance with characters I can empathize with. Our protagonist, 15 year old Kaito-kun with his glasses and fluffy hair. Also there's the main heroine Ichika-senpai, also with glasses, but we'll talk about her in another section, let's focus on main-character-kun for now. He loves filming with his camera. He literally records everything. And everyone without consent. He's a crimina- anyways, that's kinda foreshadowing a sad ending, if you've watched enough Animes then you might get what I mean. Like, come on, how can he record literally everything and not show us a film at the end where everyone looks back and cries... (sorry for the potential spoiler but you'll still definitely tear up in the end). After a paragraph about how our 15 year old criminal is a cute protagonist, let's take a look at our side characters and their relationships, and oh wow, it's a love polygon. Each individual side character has a unique own personality which are even pretty rarely seen on Anime. Also I won't tell you why I gave 'Antagonist' 2 cheeky points because that'd for real be a spoiler this time, so go watch it now, or after reading my review. The story itself originates from the Anime Onegai Teacher, written by the same Author. I believe that it differs in some way, but since I've never actually watched Onegai Teacher, I should stay shut. Yeah 14/15 points for writing is fair, that's all.
The music, something that still makes me feel nostalgic, and probably will, for a long time. The opening theme as well as the ending theme both represent the anime quite well (also bangers) and the OST is quite nice as well. Honestly nothing much more to say at this point, so let's move on, to the art. Animation was rather average even though it was definitely not bad. I liked the character designs and the world seems nice, especially since it was a leitmotif (aside from the camera and the glasses, if you count that too).

Phew, finally reached the 50% checkpoint. Now let's talk about the Emotions while watching and why I felt so. The whole Anime had a brilliant Vibe. It's Summer, we meet with our friends and do something nice. We meet a new girl, we Fall in love with her. Yes, we. Some parts made my heart melt because it was so cuteeeee <3, and even though I saw the conclusion from miles away, I still teared up. Story-wise, I liked it. I did hope for a more clear ending (because the current one is kinda sneaky and only gives you a hint on the future), but overall, it was basically perfect. Your perfectly written Romance/Drama mixed with some Sci-Fi, so it's special in some way. I also enjoyed every bit of it. At least I cannot revise a boring scene on the spot. Nothing more to say. Now let's talk about the characters, or rather 2 specific ones. First up we have our Kaito-kun or Criminal-kun or whatever. I like him. He's a understandable main character. Not like in other romances, where your MC needs 3 season 2 OVAs and a movie to confess. He did it pretty early, if that's a spoiler. He also clearly decideds on one girl, arguebly the best girl. No, not just arguebly, but rather definitely. Let's switch to Takatsuki Ichika-senpai. The best girl. The one and only. The alien girl. I just love her. I cannot despise her. She's just too cute, and she and Kaito fit very well as a couple. They have such a realistic chemistry. They seek each others nearth. They do romantic things. I have no idea what romantic things are and what couples normally do because I'm lonely, but that doesn't matter, because I get to watch this cute anime because escapism is a thing, and if you're also lonely, then go watch it. You'll feel better for a moment and then realize how lonely you are after watching it. Have fun.
-Ahem, I drifted off a bit. Anyways, this Anime will forever stay a cute core memory for me. Which is why it gets 10 points for the category Memory. Makes sense.

Go watch it, if you seek a cute romance. Go watch it, if you don't. Go watch it, if you already did. Go watch it, if you are right now (??). Bye.

animes.name: Ore no Nounai Sentakushi ga, Gakuen Love Comedy wo Zenryoku de Jama Shiteiru
animes.alt_name: My Mental Choices Are Completely Interfering With My School Romantic Comedy

rating.explain:
What a wild ride this was - Ore no Nounai Sentakushi ga, Gakuen Love Comedy wo Zenryoku de Jama Shiteiru or just NouCome. After watching through all 10 episodes in just 4 hours or so, I surely have lots to say about this. I started to watch it after Renai Boukun and Rokujouma no Shinryakusha!?, and they all received my same treatment. I watched all three of them because of Youtube Compilations, they all have a similar genre, which is School/Harem/Romance/Comedy and maybe some Ecchi. I could bring some ERABE jokes, but I've already seen too many in the MAL top reviews, so I feel like that would be too unoriginal. So let's just start normally!

This Anime surely doesn't need to hide it's characters. They're all well written and they all love Amakusa Kanade, because that's just the genre. But maybe that is also the biggest problem about this type of Anime, anmely it's genre. But before I talk about the bad, let me explain my ratings for the characters. Our protagonist Kanade seemed like a weirdo to me at first, before I knew anything about the gimmick. But after watching for a while, I could empathize really well with him. And after watching even more, I couldn't empathize at all with him. His decisions are mostly natural and he's quite smart for an Anime harem protagonist. BUT SADLY he's also as dull as the average harem protagonist. He does end up with Chocolat though, who I believe is the best girl, but wether you like her or any of the other girls more, at least we got an actual ending istead of a harem ending or an open ending. Nonetheless, I do think that he's mostly a good protagonist, at least he got some balls. The antagonist got points, even though there is none. You could argue that ERABE is the one, so I just gave it 2 points out of generosity. The side characters were great, we had our girls, who all were basically never seen before, at least the more important ones. I will not talk about all the generic stuff, because as already mentioned: simply based on the genre and tags, you already know half of the cast from memory of all the other same Animes... Writing-wise it wasn't anything too revolutionary, I liked the plot but similar ones already existed, and they all tend to have the same genre and tags for some reason... (like fr, what's with the School/Harem/Romance/Comedy(/Ecchi) Combo? But that's what people like, myself included, because it's fun and easy to watch and might even stay in memory). I spoilered myself the ending (of the LN) by reading a million different posts on a million different webpages, but nobody really knows it exactly (besides maybe this, edit: I also found this to be helpful), however, from what I've read, it seemed like we got a real conclusion! (Look at the spoiler above to... spoiler yourself) Logical was the only low score in that category because there were moments where you just thought: why? Ahem, but you can also blame the genre for that. Going on, the music was decent. The OP and ED were nice but not bangers, and the VAs were rather unfamous. I still loved Kanade's voice and all his voicecracks to it's fullest! The art was average, so it got an average score (which kinda seems pretty high, but trust me, it's actually average!)

Phew, I can feel that this review will be a long one. But after watching the same thing for 4 hours with very little breaks, I have lots more to talk about. Now we've come to the more fun part. And talking about fun, this Anime was fun. It was incredible comedic and had me cracking up way too often. I basically had a perma grin on my face. But maaaybe that's the fault of another emotional factor, which is the cringe. And oh dear, it was cringy af. I honestly don't know wether it's good or bad, but it is. I'd honestly give 'Strong Emotions' 8 points or more if they managed to keep it that funny without me literally needing to pause it and roll on the floor for a minute. Literally. I did that. I know. Subjectively, the story was weak in many parts. Especially the more serious ones. And that might be connected with the characters. But before I write another 200 words paragraph about the characters and the problems I had with them, it's time for my sponsor- I mean the explanation why I gave 'Enjoyable Content' a 7, which is simply because I enjoyed it a lot (because of funny).

(Wow, I never used that many paragraphs and line breaks in one review, might want to keep it this way, so my reviews look longer and are better structured, making it an easier read)
THE CHARACTERS and how I feel about their BEHAVIOUR (not good). The biggest problem I had was the cringe. I couldn't take things seriously, but to be fair, it wasn't a deadly serious Anime. Still, some parts hurt to watch, and the one to blame the most was probably Kanade. You see, he has a hard life, forced to do whatever bs. But at some moments, he could've taken the initiative better. But then the author is like:'Oh no he won't Fall in love for 11.9 volumes because I want to milk out more content with a virgin'. I don't think I explained that well. But if you watch the Anime, you most likely will understand. Sometimes, he just feels too much like the generic 'nice guy' MC, surrounded by girls even though he's weak af and getting beaten up by guy every 2 minutes (Oh also I want to mention the one guy who's in love with Kanade's ass. Best boy). And watching a pathetic guy is rather not fun. But not only that, the behaviour from some others were illogical too. Why is Yukihira like how she is? Well I shouldn't be too harsh to people with social anxiety or I'll get cancelled on twitter, instead I'd ask myself: why is Kanade fine getting punched by her rather hefty. Also why's the teacher a legal loli, but that's also to blame on the fact that this is a harem Anime. WHICH is also an exuse for the absence of Kanade's parents or almost any parents as well as the unimportance of background characters. The others don't even ask themselfs why everything only revolves around our main cast. But as I said, blame the genre. And the author. Some scenes, which were used as comedic factors, but also to give us more information about the story, mostly end in nothing. They are mysteriously gone because of ERABE or the 'forget-last-5-minutes' thing (which is basically violence) or some unlucky encounters. I'd wish to see Chocolat (MAYBE MINOR MINOR SPOILER) stay in her normal form in ep7, but no, she just turns back in the same episode. At least the spoiler tag above will tell you more. Same with the developement of the relationship between Yukihira and Kanade, or rather the relationships between Kanade and everyone. It's just so slow, and for no good reason. But as I already mentioned, it's mostly to blame the genre. The difference is, that this time it could've been much better if there was more progress, while other Animes, such as Renai Boukun don't need that progress, because they simply don't progress (which is not a good solution, please). Or Animes like TWOGK, which might progress slowly, but the characters aren't just brain dead in love with some pathetic guy (Wow I surely wrote a lot in this paragraph).

To come to an end, I will now come to an end... My previously mentioned issues didn't contribute to the failure of the category 'Characters' under Subjective, but they demonstrate the problem within the whole Anime, which is why I couldn't bring myself to rate this any higher, even though the comedic factor was like a 9, way higher than the comedic factors of Renai Boukun or Rokujyouma. But hey, for the characters category, I still gave some points, especially because Chocolat is such a cutie. I honestly cannot even decide between her 2 states, but that only means that I'm fine with either of them, which is a good thing. And for the last category, it got a pretty high score just because of how addictive it was, since I watched it all basically in one go. The aftertaste I feel right now is huge, but sadly not positive enough for my taste. Even the LN ending didn't fully satisfy me, regardless of how well or badly-written the Anime ending was.

My final conclusion: I forgot what I wrote about for the past 40 minutes, lemme look it up again. Mhm. Oh, I remember again, just like how Chocolat did... which makes me sad. It makes me sad because this Anime was great. It was great because it was funny. It was great because it had great characters. It was great because you could watch it in one go without thinking. But I made a mistake. I thought about it, too deeply to be exact. And that ruined it for me. I cannot handle the cringe, if I know that it would just resolve in air. The romance was good, but again was it actually good or yet maybe bad, because it couldn't resolve in a satisfying ending. Overall, you should watch it if you want to laugh. But please accept the stupid actions of the characters and don't ask too many questions like I did. Thank you. And bye (Oh that reminds me that I forgot about saying bye in my previous few reviews).

animes.name: Mahouka Koukou no Rettousei
animes.alt_name: The Irregular at Magic High School

animes.name: Ore no Nounai Sentakushi ga, Gakuen Love Comedy wo Zenryoku de Jama Shiteiru OVA
animes.alt_name: My Mental Choices Are Completely Interfering With My School Romantic Comedy OVA

rating.explain:
Before reading this review, read my NouCome season 1 review first. I mean, you'd watch the first season before this 1 episode OVA too. Also, this review is more about the LN ending than this OVA, because there's not much to say about 20 minutes of content + this is most likely the final time we'll see NouCome as an Anime so we might as well just finish it all with this one.

The story reminds me of Date A Live IV episode 1. If you haven't watched it, then here's what it is about: MC-kun is transportet in some parallel world or rather game world, but the characters strongly resembles the real life ones. Basically, all the game characters are just the real ones, but with different names and settings. The characters are therefore equaly well-written like in s1, but the MC seemed even more dull, which is why I took away a point. Music and Art didn't really change, so not a whole lot changed from s1.

Now let's talk a bit more about the problem with the messed up ending, which stands out the most in the whole 'Subjective' category (all the other points were basically the same stats as in s1 but a bit weaker). I've looked up the summaries for the latter LNs up to 12, which you can find here. There is no translated version of this, or at least I didn't find one, and sadly there most likely wont ever be one. I suggest you read the latter 2 posts. Stop reading here if you don't want any LN spoilers (though you probably wont ever read it so whatever). The ending is messed up. Kanade does end up with Chocolat, who I liked the most. But to be honest, she's definitely not the best girl for most people. And even if you love her like I do, there's still Sora. This has nothing to do with the OVA, but like I said, it's the ending of the series, just like how the OVA is the final episode of the Anime. Sora is the root of all problems, volume 11 demonstrates that. Vol. 12 was just there to clean up the mess, but even in the final scene, the confession from Kanade to Choco was disrupted by the ERABE person. Why cannot the author just make a normal ending. Oh and before that happened the series got the ReLife treatment, if you know what I mean (if you don't, then go watch ReLife, that has a much clearer and better end for sure). What I want to say is that now after I have the knowledge of the terribly messy true final ending, I cannot look at the Anime ending without thinking of the LN anymore, and that affects the score.

I recommend watching this if you enjoyed season 1. And after that I recommend reading through all the stuff I've linked, to get angry at the author. Or maybe you should not know more than what the Anime tells you, so you wont hate it. Otherwise this special episode has basically the same vibe as the original, maybe a bit less funny, but still fine. Which is why I gave it a 6/10 'fine'. Thank you for reading and bye.

animes.name: Seiken Tsukai no World Break
animes.alt_name: World Break: Aria of Curse for a Holy Swordsman

rating.explain:
I remember! I remember everything now! The reason why I'm writing this review! It's to show you the beauty of ass Animes, of course. Seiken Tsukai no World Break - or just World Break - is nothing new. It's the same as all the other Action/Harem/Ecchi/School and whatever Animes. Just like IS, Hundred, Asterisk, Chivalry, and probably more than a hundred more. They all have something in common. They all suck. But since I've basically watched them all, I find it amusing to look for more of this sort and I cannot resist binge watching everything.

World Break had 1 gimmick the previously mentioned didn't. The magic is casted via long poetry and the character were all reincarnated. That's about it. The rest is unoriginal. Like always, the story is about our hero, our protagonist, our harem-king, the one and only Haimura Moroha. He was introduced as a weakling, but because of his many previous lifes, as well as plot armor, he becomes stronger and eventually the strongest. He is the center of attention, without him the story wouldn't exist. Sadly enough, he's kinda meh, average harem protagonist. But because of the harem-ending everyone can be happy I guess. Our side characters are mostly generic as well, however I liked Shizuno and Maya. The others aren't even worth mentioning though. The ending of this was pretty rushed and the storytelling illogical at times, but overall, it's more or less your average harem story, with a nice idea but not too much depth. The animation was lackluster and just bad. The many poetries in the magic spells and in the OSTs I believe, gave the Anime a strange feeling, but before we talk about that, why do they stand around for decades and let their opponent say their spell? I don't get it.

Now we come to the music and the poetries. I decided that those categories were major roles how the vibe was presented, so we talk about them in this subjective section. I love the music. They sound creepy and melancholic, but also heroic and divine. It reminds me of fairy tales (I mean like actual fairy tales, not the Anime Fairy Tail). I especially liked the OST Dragon Heart, which was always played whenever Haimura remembered something. World Break was funny at times and enjoyable at some other times, but it tried incredibly hard to be actually good. That only worked out for me for as long as the music was playing. Because that's one of the only things I can take seriously in this Anime. But sometimes that much is enough, enough to convince me that this is actually not that bad. In fact, I'd say, for your average Anime of this type, this might as well be above average. But because of the flawed characters and story, I cannot rate this any higher. I did watch it all in 2 goes, and it did catch my attention with all the holy songs, so it'll get some points for that.

I don't recommend this to everyone, but just to those, who seek more of Animes of this type. If you like harem action school Animes, then go watch this, it will sure bless you and curse you simultaneously, just like how Moroha is kinda split into two halfs. Don't expect a high quality Anime, but rather something trashy, that tries to be serious with it's good aspects such as the poems and strange vibe.

I.....WILL DEFINITELY NOT FORGIVE ANYONE WHO WILL TAKE RATING POINTS TWO FROM ME!!!!

Rokujouma no Shinryakusha!? - I started this because I saw a clip from the first episode in a YouTube Anime compilation. And it was 100% worth it.

The first episode introduced our main cast. And it was hilarious. After watching that, I decided to binge through all of it. What I think is the most outstanding factor, is the variety of different unique characters and their interactions with each other, even though we have such a simple and repetitive concept, where every girl from the main cast has mysteriously Fallen in love with Koutarou, who also appears to be the cause of all trouble. I really want to point out the individuell characters. First up we have Koutarou, and he is normal, but a good normal. I once read something in a MAL review which said that he was a normal guy with strengths and weaknesses and pride, which essentially makes him a good protagonist. I cannot agree more. Kasagi Shizuka is our next character. She's rather unimportant in the beginning but gets to shine later on more. She always has hilarious moments when someone breaks her apartment number 106. She can also fight ghosts and golems with her bare hands for some reason (?). Our first Invader is Sanae, arguebly one of the best girls. Such a cute and wholesome ghost, always by Koutarous side. Then we have arguebly the best character from the series: our favourite cosplayer. Yurika is such a great character. Her incredible clumsyness makes her just too adorable and I love her voice (also funny to imagine that it's the same VA as Yurine from Jashin-chan, since those 2 characters are the exact opposite). Yurika Fai-to! I want to skip Kiriha because there's nothing special about her and I'll skip Tulip because I cannot even spell her real name correctly. Ruth however, is another great character. She has her own gimmick, or rather, inside joke with her stag beetles, where she turns into... Yor mode??? And the best for the last, Harumi-senpai. The heroine we needed, the best girl. Well, that's it, she's just the best girl, I got nothing more to say about her. Each of those fractions, who fight over room 106 have their own reasons and individual enemies. Yet they all together make 106 what 106 fundamentally is. Without one of them, it's just not the same. Storywise, this is about where the good ends. Sadly, most episodes are filled with generic harem and fanservice plot, but hey, at least the characters are great. I cannot say a whole lot to animation other then it was pretty good, but nothing special. However, I want to point out that the voice actors where stacked, like absolutely cracked. They all did a fairly good job, but other than me liking them, there is not much to say about VAs. The opening was fine, the ending slaps on another level though. Also look up the MV please. It is literally perfect. The milk tea theme together with the Korama and Karama cosplay. I love it.

Now let's talk about subjective, even though I basically already said everything. There where many hilarious moments and the feeling while watching was great. It wasn't noticible cringe but instead had me rolling on the floor. Some climatic moments could've been more climatic. Cosplayer. The Story had it's flaws but overall it was very enjoyable. The ending was neither satisfying or unsatisfying, since it wasn't even finished. As far as I know, the Anime adapted 7 out of 35 light novels, which are still ongoing. Apparently, the main plot is already done but the romance stuff with all the characters is still ongoing. Yes, this light novel series has been ongoing since 2009. I'd really want to know who Koutarou will end up with (Harumi pls author-sama). Yurika Fai-to! The characters (especially Yurika) where lovable and ungeneric. Before I forget it, I gotta point out the true VIPs, Korama and Karama. They are the best thing about Kiriha and they had stacked VAs. They're super cute and watchign them is hella fun. We had many unexpected moments with our cast, and that is something where you just need the right characters for it to feel good. Cosplayer. It will stay in my memory for some time, that much is sure.

An 8/10, 'Very Good' feels right, which is why I rated it that. Don't forget to bully Cosplayer Yurika (for legal reasons, bullying is not appropriate and you should restrain from doing so, even if she's a cosplayer trying to convince you that she's actually a magical girl).

rating.explain:
'They call it Hundred because you've seen this show hundreds of times already.' - top MAL review from LeaderTGW. I love watching each and every sci-fi harem ecchi action school anime. They're all the same. Asterisk, Chivalry, IS, or now this: Hundred. No idea why I'm forcing myself to write about this. But here we go:

I really do not want to write too much about it and I also forgot basically everything that happened, because nothing was worth remembering. The characters are plain, and even below average in the bottom league. The only good thing about Hayato besides his name is the fact that he actually decides on one girl. Emile is also fairly cute, but all the others were just plain and dumb and aggresive and thirty for Hayato for no real reason (well that's kinda just part of this genre, so I'll let that pass). The story itself isn't worth mentioning, so I wont (quality review for quality anime). Basically it's about... I have no idea. The opening was rather... special, not bad but not great either. And the animation budget was also rather on the short side this time.

I was mostly not amused by this, but I do remember 1 thing. In the first episode, Hayato transfered to the new school, battling with the Queen of the school. I knew instantly, what will happen. Either he wins, or he loses but should've won. Regardless of what happens, the queen would Fall in love with him for no reason and he'll be the star of the school (or rarely the opposite). After that, the high class guys all keep an eye on mr. Irregular. Yeah that's all I remembered. I've seen such things way too often already and I'm still looking forward to watch more. For no explainable reason, I also binged this in one day. Don't ask me why, I probably couldn't procastinate this trash any longer, other than Kanojo, Okarishimasu. I don't have anything other to say. It was not worth my time.

Actually, it was worth my time, because my life goal is obviously going to be to watch all those anime genres. I just love watching the bottom of the trash. But it's not worth your time, if you're not like me, which I highly doubt, so my advise: avoid this at all cost.

rating.explain:
Don't take this show too seriously, it's a parody. And a pretty reasonable one as well. Of course I am talking about Renai Boukun, your above average average Harem Anime. Renai Boukun is about... well I don't really know, but at least it's amusing. Before I watched, I did not expect anything special, but it turned out to be better than I thought. Also kinda funny that I'm writing this review after my Rokujyoma review, since I watched both Animes because of a Youtube Anime Compilation.

Our main character - namely Aino Seiji, is more or less your average guy. But hey, he had enough courage to kiss girls and doesn't go red instantly when getting near his girlfriends. Then we have his entire Harem, including his sister as well as the antagonist for some reason (Japan why?). I especially loved Akane, but the others where all written just fine. Akane is a great Yandere, crazy enough to stab people, but not crazy enough to not appear cute. There also are some funsies character because this is still just a Death Note Harem Parody, like the cat angel or the penguing... thing. Poor Akua. Also if you're planning to watch it, expect to hear 'Aino' or 'Seiji' every 10 to 15 seconds, because, that's just how it is. This story isn't really a story either, mostly because it's still not a serious masterpiece. But there is progression in the story (wow I've been contradictive lately, just like Guri), which resulted into nothing at the end, really, where is season 2. Many things were left unexplained and many supernatural events were occuring, but I guess that's how you create a funny comedy. The opening song was very dissonant in some parts, which I loved, since that's how the resolving part sounds better. Ending was not bad as well, and the VAs were kinda stacked for this kind of Anime. We had Rie Takahashi, Ono Kensho and Satou Rina (who voiced Yuzu's mom and I loved that character). The animation was average at most, but I did like the world and the character designs. Especially from Kami-sama.

This Anime is something to laugh at, and I did. There were many hilarious moments, wether with some stupid looking angels, or stupid God's, or just the illogical things that are flashing by. Too many things happen at once, nothing is predictable (well, besides maybe some scenes which were literally copied from your How-To-harem-Textbook). Some characters I'd like to point out: First of all Akane. I gave her a point for being a waifu because she's your perfect yandere. I didn't have any yanderes on my waifu tierlist before so... it's my first time. Ahem, she's a great comedic factor, always willingly to kill everyone for our Seiji-kun. And the rest of her family isn't much different (I lied). I'll skip over Yuzu but HOLY YUZU'S MOM is a fantastic character as well. Because she just is. And then there's Coraly, the cat thing, and Stolas, the penguin thing. I don't really know why they exist, but I'm happy that they exist. Lastly, we have Kami-sama, the true best daddy. Honestly I'm speechless for how well he fits into this whole thing which I cannot even call a story. Before I forget it: the story is about a kiss note, basically a death note but you get the point. And after a few episodes it's about a kiss phone. And at some point it was banished completely (?). Yeah I'm outta here.

Did you know that there are some angels SO beautiful, that you'll die when you simply look at them?

rating.explain:
Princess Connect! Re:Dive Season 2 (short: Pricon s2) - the Anime you'll probably find first when sorting for the highest overall score. This is truly the masterpiece of all masterpieces and I will tell you why this is a must-watch for everyone. First of all, this is the second season of Pricon series, a game-sourced Anime made by the studio CygamesPictures. And oh boy, who would've thought that they'd produce this masterpiece. The first season might be a bit 'boring' at times, however I'd strongly suggest you to finish it, because what comes after is more than rewarding.

Let's start chronologically, with our Characters, or more precisely, our protagonists Yuuki and his guild. They're all very different characters, all with their own background and personalities, yet they came together to eat the most yummy food they can find. If you've watched Pricon s1 (which you should do btw, just so you can watch this), then you already know them. They didn't change a whole lot, I have to say. There's still some character developement though, don't worry. This time we also get more to see from the antagonist, who happens to be a white haired cat girl. The Anime also has a variety of side characters, all with their own charm and depths. Aside from the characters, who you'll get to know soon enough, let's talk a bit about the story. It's and adventurous Anime with some game lore, and friendship, with some great fight scenes. But that's all. There's no 'real' depth. However, I have to say that it doesn't need any more depth, since it would get even more complex than it already is, due to the lack of context if you haven't played the game (like me). I can say that the Story is one of the weakest points, still only 3 points behind the maximum. So basically, the Anime does great in the category it does worst. That tells a lot already. Up next is the Music and this is where this Anime really stands out to the rest. They decided to reuse the recognizable theme from S1 and the game, which is a banger at least. It's in my top 5 list of songs overall, and the ending is beautiful and sweet as well, so that maybe explains the 8 in OP/ED. As OST, they used different variations of the main theme, and since the main theme is a banger, the OST is as well.Continuing, the Animation, and oh dear... I had to give max points + bonus again. The character movement was smooth, the landscapes beautiful and the fight scenes incredible. Especially in ep9, CyGames really reached ufotable level there. I also had to give some credit to the World Building, which is why Animation 'only' got 7 points. Finalizing the Objective half we can safely say, writing was the worst part but still not bad, and music + animation made up for it as well.

Switching to the Subjective side now: First up we have the Emotions, the one and only thing to make anyone biased, and I am. You see, I just felt a lot, happyness, sadness and suspense. There were some special moments or special episodes, where you might hear your own heart pounding, but you don't actually hear it because the OST is such a banger so you turn up the volume. The Vibe carried as well, since every episode felt so refreshing. You just cannot dislike the characters, their relation, and what they're doing. My subjective view on the story is not much different. My score for a satisfying ending might seem low, but in reality it just got shot down by the other 2 criterias in that category, in which both this anime exceeds very well. Then we have my personal view on the characters. Nothing much to say here, other than 'I like the characters' and I don't have a waifu here. I conclude my rating with full points for memory, since, well... it's my number 1 rated Anime, of course it will stay in my head without rent for quite some time. Go watch Pricon S1 now if you haven't and what are you even doing if you haven't watched S2 after you watched S1. Bye.
